articles.title: Microsoft’s first advanced reasoning AI is here
articles.description: Microsoft announced a bunch of new in-house AI models at Build 2026, including a new "flagship" model: MAI-Thinking-1. It's an ambitious step into model development for Microsoft, which introduced its initial in-house models last year - before then, it had relied on OpenAI's models. The two companie
articles.content: Microsoft announced a bunch of new in-house AI models at Build 2026, including a new "flagship" model: MAI-Thinking-1. It's an ambitious step into model development for Microsoft, which introduced its initial in-house models last year - before then, it had relied on OpenAI's models. The two companies recently renegotiated their deal to loosen ties. According to Microsoft , MAI-Thinking-1 is a "medium-sized model" that "matches leading models" on "key" software engineering benchmarks. Microsoft s
articles.author: Jay Peters

articles.description: Opal, the company famous for making a fancy webcam, has pivoted to making other consumer electronics. Fueled by big investments from OpenAI and Samsung, it’s working on an audio gadget first.

How small businesses can leverage AI From accounting to design to market research and product development, there’s a staggering breadth of skills need
articles.content: This is today’s edition of The Download , our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. How small businesses can leverage AI From accounting to design to market research and product development, there’s a staggering breadth of skills needed to run a business. Large companies can hire experts to handle these tasks, but small businesses don’t always have that luxury. That’s where AI comes in. Today’s models can already take on a range
articles.author: Thomas Macaulay

articles.title: How small businesses can leverage AI

From accounting to design to market research and product development, there’s a staggering breadth of skills needed to run a bu
articles.content: This article is from Making AI Work , MIT Technology Review ’s limited-run newsletter examining how to apply LLMs across industries. To receive it in your inbox, sign up here . From accounting to design to market research and product development, there’s a staggering breadth of skills needed to run a business. A large company can hire experts to handle these tasks, but small businesses don’t always have this luxury. That’s where AI comes in. Today’s AI models do a decent job at these tasks. The
articles.author: Peter Hall

articles.title: Rehumanizing global health care with agentic AI
articles.description: The global health care sector is under increasing strain.  Decades of chronic underinvestment and constraints in recruitment have coincided with a surge in demand for services for aging populations. Gaps in provision are already taking a toll, with fragmented access to care and high rates of st
articles.content: The global health care sector is under increasing strain. Decades of chronic underinvestment and constraints in recruitment have coincided with a surge in demand for services for aging populations. Gaps in provision are already taking a toll, with fragmented access to care and high rates of stress and burnout among staff. And it’s getting worse. The World Health Organization has warned that current shortfalls will increase to 11 million workers by 2030. In their urgent hunt for a solution, many
articles.author: MIT Technology Review Insights

articles.title: Trump signs executive order to review AI models before they’re released
articles.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order Tuesday creating a "voluntary framework" for AI companies to share their frontier models with the federal government before they're released "to promote secure innovation and strengthen the cybersecurity of critical infrastructure." The order says the
articles.content: President Donald Trump signed an executive order Tuesday creating a "voluntary framework" for AI companies to share their frontier models with the federal government before they're released "to promote secure innovation and strengthen the cybersecurity of critical infrastructure." The order says the US AI industry has succeeded in part "because we refuse to stifle this innovation with overly burdensome regulation," but that it also recognizes new AI capabilities come with security risks. Accordi
articles.author: Lauren Feiner

articles.title: GitHub Copilot app: The agent-native desktop experience
articles.description: At Microsoft Build 2026, GitHub introduced new tools, updates, and surfaces so agents can work the way you already work. The post GitHub Copilot app: The agent-native desktop experience appeared first on The GitHub Blog .
articles.content: While the agentic shift has made development faster, it’s also led to disjointed workflows, more context switching, and too much time spent reviewing agent-generated code. If agents are going to be a durable part of how software gets built, they need a real place in the developer workflow. Yet most developer tools were not designed for directing multiple agents in parallel. Context scatters across windows. You lose track of what’s running. Code lands in pull requests without a clear
articles.author: Natalie Guevara

articles.title: The durable, floatable UE Wonderboom 4 speaker is cheaper than ever
articles.description: I like the UE Wonderboom 4 Bluetooth speaker so much that I bought two of them, though I wish I could have paid less for them. Fortunately, you can stock up on the cheap, so long as you like the color blue. The Wonderboom 4 is available in blue at Amazon for $51.49, and at […]
articles.content: I like the UE Wonderboom 4 Bluetooth speaker so much that I bought two of them, though I wish I could have paid less for them. Fortunately, you can stock up on the cheap, so long as you like the color blue. The Wonderboom 4 is available in blue at Amazon for $51.49, and at Walmart and Best Buy for $51.99 ($28 off its regular price). That’s the lowest price we’ve seen yet for the waterproof speaker, with most of the other color options selling at a slightly higher (but still discounted) $69.99. U
articles.author: Brad Bourque

articles.title: Google rolls out fake call detection to protect against AI deepfake impersonation scams
articles.description: As people increasingly refuse to answer calls from unknown numbers, scammers are shifting their tactics by spoofing trusted phone numbers and using AI deepfake technology to sound like authority figures, family members, or employers.

articles.title: title: "I Revived Wrisha — the Emotional AI Companion I Left for Dead" published: false tags: githubchallenge, devchallenge, ai, python cover_image:
articles.description: What is Wrisha? Wrisha is a desktop emotional AI companion — an animated character who can see you, hear you, talk back, and react. The pipeline is genuinely multimodal: Vision — webcam + facial-emotion detection (OpenCV / FER) Hearing — speech-to-text so you can just talk to her Brain — an LLM gene
articles.content: What is Wrisha? Wrisha is a desktop emotional AI companion — an animated character who can see you, hear you, talk back, and react. The pipeline is genuinely multimodal: Vision — webcam + facial-emotion detection (OpenCV / FER) Hearing — speech-to-text so you can just talk to her Brain — an LLM generates her replies, in-character Voice — text-to-speech with mood-modulated tone Avatar — an animated face (pygame) that emotes and lip-syncs I built the bones of it a while back, got busy, and walked

articles.content: We're expecting Android 17 to begin rolling out later this month, but first, Google has a batch of updates for the wider Android device ecosystem. As usual, some of the new features are limited to specific devices, and others require using Google's apps. But if you don't mind the latter, you can get automated protection from the growing threat of deepfake phone scams. According to Google, "impersonation fraud" is one of the most common types of financial scams. The FTC tracked almost $3 billion
articles.author: Ryan Whitwam

articles.description: My father worked in IT for over thirty years, and growing up around that shaped how I thought about computers. The earliest memory I have is sitting in my father's lap as he does something on his computer. One of the oldest photos I have is of me sitting on a chair in front of a computer. I grew up
articles.content: My father worked in IT for over thirty years, and growing up around that shaped how I thought about computers. The earliest memory I have is sitting in my father's lap as he does something on his computer. One of the oldest photos I have is of me sitting on a chair in front of a computer. I grew up idolizing him. I switched to Linux when I was 12, by myself. I taught myself scripting, picked up programming basics, and spent more time in a terminal than most adults I knew. articles.description: Neste projeto (hand on), assumindo o papel de Especialista Cloud com a responsabilidade de conduzir a migração automatizada de usuários e gerenciar os recursos do IAM (Identity and Access Management) na plataforma AWS. Com um total de 100 usuários a serem migrados, foi essencial garantir a ativação
articles.content: Neste projeto (hand on), assumindo o papel de Especialista Cloud com a responsabilidade de conduzir a migração automatizada de usuários e gerenciar os recursos do IAM (Identity and Access Management) na plataforma AWS. Com um total de 100 usuários a serem migrados, foi essencial garantir a ativação do MFA (Autenticação de Múltiplos Fatores) em suas contas, seguindo as melhores práticas de segurança. Para evitar a repetição tediosa de tarefas manuais na console da AWS, concentrei-me na automação
articles.author: Luis Cruz

articles.title: Why a Neo Geo port of Doom is functionally impossible
articles.description: Sprite-based graphics architecture makes first-person 3D a challenge.
articles.content: Here at Ars, we've taken pleasure in reporting on versions of Doom that run on everything from wireless earbuds and printers to Windows' notepad.exe and even inside Doom itself . So when we hear that a piece of game-playing hardware from the '90s (or later) can't run Doom , our ears perk up. That hardware is the Neo Geo, an early '90s game console that players of a certain age will remember for its eye-watering launch price and its relatively strong pixel-pushing power for the time. Despite that
articles.author: Kyle Orland

articles.title: Number of suspected Ebola cases falls by hundreds as testing ramps up
articles.description: The number of cases falls from 1,100 to 437 with increased testing.
articles.content: The estimated size of the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo has fallen by hundreds of cases as outbreak response efforts have ramped up and increased testing has ruled out illnesses. On Tuesday, a representative for the World Health Organization confirmed to Reuters that Congolese authorities are now reporting 437 cases in the DRC, including 321 confirmed cases and 116 suspected. That's a significant difference from the case count the WHO relayed Friday , which totaled 1,041
articles.author: Beth Mole
